problem: Dispatcher leases and plans but doesn't execute. Auto-review and auto-ship exist but git ops are manual. Completed lanes aren't released. Shape-to-queue requires manual work-sync. The system prepares everything but nobody picks up the work.
constraints: Claude Code Agent tool for spawning — no subprocess management
globalDecisions: Always team mode — even single tasks get a spawned agent
Supervisor is a Claude session, not a Python process. Python owns state, Claude owns execution.
Two layers of agent spawning: feature-level (executor) and task-level (implementer)
Executor never writes code — purely orchestration. Implementers are the only code-writing agents.
Git ops handled by executor when profile allows full auto-ship
Execution events are the observability primitive

candidateFeatures.userOutcome: Every layer (supervisor, executor, implementer) writes structured events to a shared append-only log. loop-status and loop-history CLI commands surface real-time state and recent history.
candidateFeatures.scopeSummary: Shipped. execution_events.py with log/read/heartbeat/render. CLI: loop-status, loop-history.
candidateFeatures.handoffSummary: Create .cnogo/execution-log.jsonl format. Add log_execution_event(actor, feature, event, data) to a new execution_events.py module. Add loop-status and loop-history to workflow_memory.py. Define agent-heartbeat-task-N.json format. Wire heartbeat polling into executor design.

candidateFeatures.displayName: Auto-Queue from Shape and Auto-Release Lanes
candidateFeatures.userOutcome: Dispatcher scans SHAPE.json for ready candidates and auto-creates Work Orders. Completed lanes are automatically released and worktrees cleaned up.
candidateFeatures.scopeSummary: Shipped. auto_queue_from_shape() + release_completed_lanes() wired into dispatch_ready_work().
candidateFeatures.handoffSummary: Add auto_queue_from_shape(root) scanning docs/planning/work/ideas/*/SHAPE.json for ready candidates. Check if Work Order exists; if not, call sync_work_order() to create queued order. Add release_completed_lanes(root) that finds lanes where latest run ship.status=completed, calls release_feature_lane(), optionally removes worktree. Wire both into dispatch_ready_work() before and after the main lease loop.

candidateFeatures.userOutcome: Each feature lane gets an executor agent that drives the full task lifecycle: spawn implementers per task frontier, wait for completion, merge worktrees, run plan-verify, handle git ops. The executor is the team leader for one feature.
candidateFeatures.scopeSummary: Create execute-lane.md agent definition. Executor reads run state, computes task frontier, spawns implementer agents per runnable task, waits for returns, calls run-task-complete/fail, triggers worktree merge, runs plan-verify, handles git commit/push/PR when profile allows.
risks: Agent context window limits may constrain long-running features with many tasks
Merge conflicts between task branches may require resolver agent escalation
candidateFeatures.handoffSummary: Create .claude/agents/executor.md with prompt that: reads run-next for task frontier, spawns Agent(implementer) per runnable task, processes results via run-task-complete/fail, triggers session-merge for multi-task results, runs plan-verify when all done, handles git ops via ship draft. Writes execution events throughout. Polls implementer heartbeats for stale detection.

candidateFeatures.userOutcome: A long-running supervisor agent that continuously dispatches features, spawns executors, monitors progress, and releases completed lanes. The operator starts it once and the system runs autonomously.
candidateFeatures.scopeSummary: Create run-loop.md command/agent that loops: auto-queue from shape, dispatch-ready, spawn executor agents for implementing lanes, wait for completions, advance transitions (auto-review, auto-ship), release completed lanes. Writes supervisor-level execution events. Configurable tick interval.
risks: Long-running session may hit context window limits — mitigate with context compaction or periodic restart
Supervisor crash loses running agent references — mitigate by persisting agent state in lane records
candidateFeatures.handoffSummary: Create .claude/commands/run-loop.md (or agent). Loop: call auto_queue_from_shape via Bash, call dispatch_ready_work via Bash, for each implementing lane spawn Agent(executor) with run_in_background=true, get notified on completion, call release_completed_lanes. Write loop_tick events. Configurable tick via WORKFLOW.json scheduler.tickIntervalMinutes.

candidateFeatures.userOutcome: Single execution model: always spawn agents. No serial path, no mode decision, no /implement command. Executor is the only execution entry point.
candidateFeatures.scopeSummary: Remove recommend_team_mode() from bridge.py. Remove mode field distinction from delivery runs (always 'team'). Remove /implement command. Update /plan to not reference /implement. Clean up profile_mode_preference(). Update docs.
risks: Existing features with in-progress serial runs may need migration path
candidateFeatures.readinessReason: Depends on executor being functional first. Migration path for existing serial runs needs definition.
candidateFeatures.handoffSummary: Remove recommend_team_mode() and serial code paths from bridge.py, delivery_run.py, implement.py. Remove .claude/commands/implement.md. Update plan templates and commands that reference /implement. Add migration: existing serial runs continue as-is; new runs always use team mode.
